title: Docker-Based Sandboxing
concept: Spawning lightweight, temporary container environments to execute generated code isolated from the host.

exercises.prompt: What is one reason why sandboxing is important when executing code?

options.text: It allows every program to access system secrets.
It prevents malicious code from affecting the host system.

exercises.explain: Sandboxing is important because it keeps the host system safe from potentially harmful code.

cards.title: What is Docker-Based Sandboxing?
cards.body: Docker-based sandboxing uses containers to create lightweight, temporary environments for executing code without affecting the host.

cards.title: Docker Container
cards.body: A Docker container is a packaged environment where you can run applications separately from other processes on the host computer.

cards.title: Container as an Apartment
cards.body: Think of a Docker container like an apartment. Each apartment has everything needed for living, but it’s separate from others to protect them from each other.

cards.title: Using Docker for Code Execution
cards.body: When you run code inside a Docker container, it's like running it in a secured space where it can’t mess with your main computer.

cards.title: Myth: Containers Slow Down Performance
cards.body: Some think containers slow down everything, but Docker containers are actually designed to be fast and lightweight!

cards.title: Container vs Host System
cards.body: Containers isolate code execution from the host system, ensuring safety and flexibility.

right.text: A temporary, isolated space for running code.

exercises.prompt: What is a main benefit of using Docker containers for executing code?

options.text: They allow viral code to spread easily.
They require a lot of memory.

exercises.explain: Docker containers create secure environments, preventing harmful interactions with the host.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Docker containers prevent code from accessing the host system directly.
exercises.explain: True, because Docker containers isolate the execution of code from the host system.

exercises.prompt: A Docker container is a ______ environment for running applications securely.
exercises.sentence: A Docker container is a lightweight environment for running applications securely.
exercises.explain: A Docker container is designed to be a lightweight environment, minimizing resource usage.

exercises.prompt: Which is a feature of Docker-based sandboxing?

options.text: It always requires lots of physical hardware resources.
It allows code to run in isolated containers.
It prevents internet access for all applications.

exercises.explain: Docker-based sandboxing allows code to run in isolated containers, ensuring security.

exercises.prompt: Why is Docker-based sandboxing considered effective for executing code?

options.text: It uses a lot of power and resources.
It isolates execution from the host environment.

exercises.explain: Docker-based sandboxing is effective because it isolates execution from the host environment, helping to protect the system from harmful code.
